findings.evidence: `rg -n "equivSubgroupOrbitsQuotientGroup_mapOfLE|orbitRelQuotientMapOfLE" code mathlib` shows the new generic declarations occur only in this file and are used to prove the deck-specific lemma at lines 213-216.
findings.fix: Make the generic map/naturality helper private or local to the proof, and keep only the existing deck-specific `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_mapOfLE` public.
findings.issue: `TauCeti.MulAction.orbitRelQuotientMapOfLE` and `equivSubgroupOrbitsQuotientGroup_mapOfLE` expose a generic helper API unrelated to the named UniversalCovers target.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "regularSubgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_(symm|apply|map)|regularSubgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup" ...` finds only the definition at line 124 and no representative/naturality lemmas for that public equivalence.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` from the regular equivalence and add `@[simp]` regular-specialized characteristic lemmas mirroring the non-regular API.
findings.issue: The public regular-cover equivalence is marked `@[expose]` but has no regular-specific `symm_mk`, `apply_inv_smul`, `apply_smul`, or naturality lemmas, so consumers of the advertised regular API must unfold it or install the typeclass witnesses manually to use the non-regular lemmas.

summary: The deck-specific equivalence is present, but the PR still exposes implementation-level generic orbit-quotient API and leaves the regular-cover specialization without its own characteristic lemmas. Downstream regular-cover users should not have to unfold the regular equivalence or rely on generic helpers.
